front: Building: exterior has color-coded pipes — blue for air/oxygen, green for water, yellow for electricity, red for elevators and escalators
Building: design inspired by Archigram's 'Plug-In City' and Cedric Price's Fun Palace
Building: exterior escalator runs up the facade in a glass tube nicknamed 'the caterpillar'
Building: Stravinsky Fountain outside features sculptures by Niki de Saint-Phalle and Jean Tinguely
Building: houses the Musée National d'Art Moderne, a public library, and the music research center IRCAM
Building: uses 9-ton cast-steel 'gerberettes' to support long trusses
Building: Gordon Matta-Clark's Conical Intersect critiqued the perceived gentrification caused by its construction
Building: called a 'Trojan Horse' in Sanford Kwinter's Requiem: For the City at the End of the Millennium
Building: London insurance market building nicknamed the 'Inside-Out Building,' with utilities placed on the exterior
Building: contains the Lutine Bell in its underwriting room
Building: described as the 'pop Gothic' version of Gothic architecture
Building: sits across the street from Norman Foster's Willis Building in London
Building: white dome with twelve yellow support towers, each representing an hour on a clock face, symbolizing Greenwich Mean Time
Architect: met Norman Foster at Yale; together they co-founded the firm Team 4 with their future spouses Su Brumwell and Wendy Cheesman
Architect: Team 4, co-founded with Norman Foster, designed the Reliance Controls factory
Architect: King Charles III compared his plans to rebuild Paternoster Square as worse than the Luftwaffe bombings
Architect: designed the Senedd (Welsh Parliament) in Wales
Architect: designed Terminal 4 of Barajas Airport in Madrid
Architect: part of the World Trade Center redevelopment team alongside Fumihiko Maki and Norman Foster, under master architect Daniel Libeskind
